steps.title: Identify what follows the blank
steps.content: The main clause ends with the quoted definition of pleasure (“the absence of pain in the body and of trouble in the ______”). What comes after is the participial phrase positing that all life’s virtues derived from this absence, which supplements — but does not stand alone as — a new independent clause.

steps.title: Eliminate each punctuation error, then confirm the match
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'soul,” positing': A comma after the closing quotation mark correctly connects the main clause to the participial phrase; positing stays lowercase because it is not a new sentence. ✓
  • Choice B — 'soul”: positing': A colon cannot link a main clause to a participial phrase. ✗
  • Choice C — 'soul”; positing': A semicolon cannot link a main clause to a participial phrase. ✗
  • Choice D — 'soul.” Positing': The period ends the sentence prematurely, leaving Positing…absence as an unattached fragment. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
